response:
THE ART GALLERY SHOWCASES TWO DIGITAL INSTALLATIONS THAT BLEND MATHEMATICS WITH VISUAL BEAUTY.
THE FIRST PIECE USES PARAMETRIC EQUATIONS TO GENERATE DYNAMIC GEOMETRIC PATTERNS ON A LARGE SCREEN.
FOR THE PATTERN TO FORM A CLOSED LOOP, THE RATIO OF A TO B MUST BE A RATIONAL NUMBER.
LET A OVER B EQUAL P OVER Q WHERE P AND Q ARE COPRIME POSITIVE INTEGERS.
THE PHASE SHIFT PHI DOES NOT AFFECT THE CLOSURE OF THE LOOP BUT ALTERS ITS ORIENTATION.
THE PERIOD T OF THIS MOTION IS EQUAL TO TWO PI TIMES Q DIVIDED BY THE GREATEST COMMON DIVISOR OF A AND B.
WHEN A AND B ARE CO-PRIME, THE PERIOD SIMPLIFIES TO TWO PI TIMES THE DENOMINATOR OF THE RATIO.
THE SECOND INSTALLATION DISPLAYS A THREE DIMENSIONAL SURFACE DEFINED BY AN IMPLICIT FUNCTION WITH A PARAMETER LAMBDA.
THIS SURFACE BEGINS AS A SPHERE WHEN LAMBDA IS ZERO AND DEFORMS AS THE PARAMETER INCREASES OR DECREASES.
THE CRITICAL VALUES FOR LAMBDA OCCUR AT TWO AND NEGATIVE ONE WHERE THE TOPOLOGY UNDERGOES SIGNIFICANT CHANGES.
AT LAMBDA EQUALS TWO THE SURFACE PINCHES AT THE ORIGIN FORMING A SINGULAR POINT THAT SPLIT INTO TWO COMPONENTS.
WHEN LAMBDA DROPS BELOW NEGATIVE ONE THE SURFACE TRANSFORMS INTO A HYPERBOLOID WITH TWO SHEETS INSTEAD OF ONE.
THE VISUAL APPEARANCE SHIFTS FROM A SMOOTH OVAL TO A COMPLEX SHAPE WITH INTERSECTING LOOPS AS LAMBDA VARYING.
IT IS GOOD TO OBSERVE HOW ALGORITHMS CAN CREATE SUCH DIVERSE AND CAPTIVATING VISUAL EXPERIENCES FOR VIEWERS.
THE JOURNALIST CAN NOW REPORT ON THE MATHEMATICAL FOUNDATIONS THAT DRIVE THESE INNOVATIVE ARTISTIC EXPRESSIONS.
